title: Making Verbs Work
description: Learn the most important rule in German: How to conjugate regular verbs so you can actually do things!
explanation: You've seen 'sein' (to be) and 'haben' (to have). These are tricky. But most German verbs are incredibly easy and follow one perfect pattern: take the basic verb, chop off the -en at the end, and add the ending for the person. Here's how to master 'machen' (to do/make), 'kommen' (to come), and 'lernen' (to learn)!

tables.title: The Regular Verb Endings
tables.note: Tip: Remember 'e, st, t, en, t, en' to quickly recite the endings!

deep_notes.title: The Stem Rule
deep_notes.text:
The basic verb is called the Infinitive. For 'machen', you first remove '-en' to find the 'stem', which is just 'mach'. Then, just plug the stem onto the endings: 
mach + -e = mache. 
mach + -st = machst. 
mach + -t = macht. Easy process!

exercises.explanation: Der Mann (he/er) takes the -t ending: kommt!

exercises.question: Welcher Satz ist richtig?
exercises.explanation: wir takes the original infinitive word ending in -en!
ihr (you all) takes the -t ending: macht!

exercises.question: Welche Formen sind genau gleich?
exercises.explanation: Both wir and sie/Sie use the base infinitive verb with -en.

exercises.question: Jemand fragt: Wer kommt heute? Deine Antwort für dich selbst: '____ ____ heute.'
Ordne die Frage:
exercises.explanation: Questions always push the verb to the front!
